recalls.Description: Petzl America is recalling its Nomic and Ergonomic ice climbing axes due to a significant fall hazard. The affected Nomic axes (model U021AA00) have serial numbers ranging from 17L 0000000 000 to 20F 9999999 999, while the Ergonomic axes (model U022AA00) include serial numbers from 18F 0000000 000 to 21L 9999999 999. These axes feature an aluminum shaft, a steel pick, and a reinforced plastic grip, with Nomic axes in black and Ergonomic axes in orange. The serial number is located on a label on the upper part of the shaft.
recalls.Title: Petzl America Issues Recall for Nomic and Ergonomic Ice Climbing Axes Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Petzl America at 877-807-3805 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or email Returns@petzl.com. More details are available online at https://qr.petzl.com/a/recall-nomic by selecting "Safety Recall" at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Petzl Nomic Ice Climbing Axes
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 4,200 (In addition, about 1,160 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Nomic Ice Climbing Axe
Recalled Ergonomic Ice Climbing Axe
Location of serial number
Recalled Nomic and Ergonomic ice climbing axes

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 26 reports of the ice axe shafts breaking at the handle. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at REI, Moosejaw Mountaineering, and various specialty retailers across the country, as well as online at petzl.com, REI.com, and Backcountry.com from December 2017 to March 2022, priced between $380 and $440.

Importers.Name: Petzl America, Inc., of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Risk of ice axe shaft breaking at the handle during use, leading to potential falls and serious injuries or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled axes immediately and contact Petzl America for a complimentary repair kit. They can register at https://www.petzl.com/US/en/Sport/Contact?request=product-recall to obtain a pre-paid shipping label for returning the shaft. Petzl America will provide a repair kit that includes a new shaft, grip rest hardware, and assembly instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: Petzl America recalls Nomic and Ergonomic ice climbing axes due to a fall hazard. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: The recall affects the Amazon Basics Camping Folding Pocket Knife featuring a 3.5-inch blade made from black high carbon stainless steel, equipped with a pocket clip. The product's model number is B0DM5BJK3H, and specific manufacturing lot codes (MLC) C25021766FJO, C25090466FJO, or C25090566FJO can be found on the packaging box.
recalls.Title: Amazon Recalls Amazon Basics Camping Folding Pocket Knives Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Amazon at 855-215-5857 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday, or visit https://amazonfoldingkniferecall.expertinquiry.com/ or www.Amazon.com and navigate to "Recalls and Product Safety Alerts" for further details.

Products.Name: Amazon Basics Camping Folding Pocket Knife
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,840

Images.Caption: Recalled Amazon Basics Camping Folding Pocket Knife
Recalled Amazon Basics Camping Folding Pocket Knife

Injuries.Name: Amazon received one report of the blade not remaining properly secured in the closed position. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from August 2025 to February 2026 for approximately $3.

Importers.Name: Amazon.com Services LLC, of Seattle, Washington

Hazards.Name: Potential laceration hazard due to folding mechanism failure

Remedies.Name: Stop using the knife immediately and register for the recall at https://amazonfoldingkniferecall.expertinquiry.com/. A prepaid return kit and instructions will be provided for returning the knife, and customers will receive a full refund or an Amazon gift card.

recalls.metaDescription: Amazon recalls Camping Folding Pocket Knives due to a laceration risk. Stop using and register for a refund today.

recalls.Description: Ozark Trail Tabletop 1-Burner Butane Camping Stoves, model number BG2247A1, are being recalled due to serious fire and burn hazards. These stoves are dark green with an orange "Ozark Trail" logo on the front, and the model number can be found on a gray label inside the fuel compartment. Consumers should be aware that these stoves may explode or ignite unexpectedly, posing significant safety risks.
recalls.Title: Recall of Ozark Trail Tabletop 1-Burner Butane Camping Stoves Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more details, contact Walmart at 800-925-6278 from 7 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT, or visit www.walmart.com/help or https://corporate.walmart.com/recalls.

Products.Name: Ozark Trail Tabletop 1-Burner Butane Camping Stove
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 201,000

Images.Caption: Recalled Ozark Trail Tabletop 1-Burner Butane Camping Stove, model BG2247A1
Recalled Ozark Trail Tabletop 1-Burner Butane Camping Stove, model BG2247A1

Injuries.Name: China Window Industry Co., Ltd. has received 26 reports of the camping stoves exploding or catching fire, including 16 reports of injuries such as second-degree burns.

Manufacturers.Name: China Window Industry Co., Ltd, of Taipei, Taiwan

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart locations nationwide and online at Walmart.com from March 2023 to October 2025, priced between $8 and $45

Hazards.Name: Explosion and fire risk

Remedies.Name: Return the stove to any Walmart store for a full refund

recalls.metaDescription: Ozark Trail Tabletop 1-Burner Butane Camping Stoves recalled due to fire hazards. Return for a full refund at Walmart.

recalls.Description:
C.A.M.P. is recalling Nimbus Lock Carabiners available in Gun Metal/Green, Gun Metal/Fuchsia, and Gun Metal/Blue. These carabiners were sold individually or as part of several packs, including the Nimbus Lock 3 Pack and the Energy Nova Pack. The recall specifically affects the carabiners, which bear batch numbers 10 23, 12 23, 02 24, 09 24, 10 24, 12 24, 01 25, 02 25, and 06 25, located on the side of the carabiner's bottom.

The issue arises from the carabiner's automatic closing mechanism, which may fail, preventing the gate from closing properly. This defect poses a serious risk of injury or death due to potential falls. C.A.M.P. encourages consumers to take immediate action regarding this recall.
recalls.Title: C.A.M.P. Issues Recall for Nimbus Lock Carabiners Due to Fall Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact C.A.M.P. toll-free at 877-421-2267 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email warranties@camp-usa.com. More information is available at www.camp-usa.com/nimbus-lock-safety-recall-information or by visiting www.camp-usa.com and selecting 'Safety Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Nimbus Lock Carabiners
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 12,600 (In addition, about 2,100 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Nimbus Lock Carabiners
Recalled Nimbus Lock Carabiner with location of batch number
Recalled Nimbus Lock Carabiners in the Nimbus Lock 3 Pack and the Locker Draw KS
Recalled Nimbus Lock Carabiners in the Energy CR3 Pack and the Energy Nova Pack

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 21 reports of the closing mechanism breaking. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: C.A.M.P. has received 21 reports of the closing mechanism failure, but no injuries have been reported.

Importers.Name: C.A.M.P. USA Inc., of Golden, Colorado

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to fall from malfunctioning closing mechanism.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled Nimbus Lock Carabiners and contact C.A.M.P. They will need to complete a recall form at www.camp-usa.com/nimbus-lock-safety-recall-registration-form, print a prepaid return shipping label, and return the product. C.A.M.P. will inspect the returned items and send back those that pass inspection. Units that fail inspection will be replaced at no cost.

recalls.metaDescription: C.A.M.P. recalls Nimbus Lock Carabiners due to a serious fall hazard. Immediate action is required for safety.

recalls.Description: Leatherman has issued a recall for its Charge Plus and Charge Plus TTi multi-tools due to a potential laceration hazard. The Charge Plus models are available in black and stainless-steel finishes, accompanied by either a black MOLLE or black nylon sheath. The Charge Plus TTi models are sold in stainless steel with a black nylon sheath, and all products feature the etched name 'Leatherman'.
recalls.Title: Leatherman Charge Plus Multi-Tools Recalled Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Leatherman at 800-847-8665 from 6 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email charge@leatherman.com, or visit www.leatherman.com/pages/charge-recall for more details.

Products.Name: Leatherman Charge Plus Multi-Tool
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 17,000

Images.Caption: Recalled Leatherman Charge Plus (stainless steel)
Recalled Leatherman Charge Plus (black)
DRecalled Leatherman Charge Plus TTi (stainless steel)
An exposed blade tip protruding from the handle

Injuries.Name: None reported

Manufacturers.Name: Leatherman Tool Group Inc., of Portland, Oregon

Retailers.Name: Available at REI, various hardware and cutlery stores across the country, as well as online at Leatherman.com, Amazon.com, and REI.com from July 2024 to March 2025, priced between $150 and $200.

Hazards.Name: Knife blade tip may not fully retract, creating a laceration hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled multi-tools and visit www.leatherman.com/pages/charge-recall to check if their product is affected. No proof of purchase is required, and a pre-paid shipping label will be provided for the return of the recalled items for free repairs.

recalls.metaDescription: Leatherman recalls Charge Plus multi-tools due to laceration risk. Check if your tool is affected and learn how to return it for free repairs.

recalls.Description: Mammut Sports Group is recalling the Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata Sets, which are designed for mountain and rock climbing. These sets feature a blue cord and an orange and gray carabiner. The recall specifically includes model numbers 2040-02840, 2040-02850, and 2040-02870, which can be located on a tag inside the pouch.
recalls.Title: Mammut Sports Group Issues Recall for Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata Sets Due to Fall Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Mammut Sports Group at 802-800-2593 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email warranty.usa@mammut.com or visit https://www.mammut.com/us/en and click on the "Recall for Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata" banner, or go to https://mammut.dani-o.com/voluntary-recall-mammut-skywalker-pro-via-ferrata-set for more details.

Products.Name: Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata Sets
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 580 (In addition, about 42 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata
Recalled Skywalker Pro Turn Via Ferrata
Recalled Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata Package
Style Number Location

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available at Jackson Hole Mountain Resort in Jackson Hole, Wyoming, Campsaver in Millville, Utah, and various outdoor and recreation retailers across the country from February 2023 to March 2025, priced between $150 and $280.

Importers.Name: Mammut Sports Group Inc., of Williston, Vermont

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to fall hazard from carabiner malfunction.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the Via Ferrata Sets immediately and contact Mammut Sports Group for a full refund. Return labels will be provided for the return of recalled products.

recalls.metaDescription: Mammut recalls Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata Sets due to fall hazard. Stop using immediately and contact for refunds.

recalls.Description: Igloo has expanded its recall to include an additional 130,000 units of the 90 Qt. Flip & Tow Rolling Coolers, manufactured before January 2024. These coolers, identifiable by the date of manufacture printed on the bottom, feature a tow handle and have "IGLOO" displayed on their sides. Various color combinations are available. The recall affects specific models with tow handles, with model numbers found on labels located at the bottom of the cooler. Models affected include the LATITUDE 90 QT ROLLER and MAXCOLD LATITUDE 90 QT ROLLER produced between July 2020 and February 2023.
recalls.Title: Igloo Expands Recall of 90 Qt. Rolling Coolers Due to Amputation and Crushing Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Igloo at 888-943-5182 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email igloo90qt@sedgwick.com, or visit www.igloo90qtrecall.expertinquiry.com or http://www.igloocoolers.com and select "Recall Information" for further details.

Products.Name: Igloo 90 Qt. Flip & Tow Rolling Coolers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 130,000 additional coolers (In addition, about 20,000 in Canada and 5,900 in Mexico) (More than one million Igloo coolers were previously recalled in February 2025)

Images.Caption: Model 34692: Recalled Igloo 90 Qt. Flip & Tow Rolling Cooler in Ash Gray Body, Aegean Sea Lid, Black Tow Handle
Model 34790: Recalled Igloo 90 Qt. Flip & Tow Rolling Cooler in Ash Gray Body, Aegean Sea Lid, White Tow Handle
Model 34785 (Same product as previously recalled Model 49937): Recalled Igloo 90 Qt. Flip & Tow Rolling Cooler in White Body and Lid
Manufacturing Date Code
Location of Manufacturing Date Code under the recalled Igloo 90 Qt. Flip & Tow Rolling Cooler

Injuries.Name: Since the recall was announced in February 2025, Igloo has received 78 reports of fingertip injuries, including 26 resulting in fingertip amputations, bone fractures or lacerations.

Manufacturers.Name: Igloo Products Corp., of Katy, Texas

Retailers.Name: Available at Costco, Target, Academy, Dick's Sporting Goods, and other retailers across the U.S., as well as online at Amazon.com and Igloocoolers.com from January 2019 to January 2025, priced between $80 and $140.

Hazards.Name: Fingertip amputation and crushing risks due to the tow handle pinching fingers

Remedies.Name: Stop using the coolers and contact Igloo for a complimentary replacement handle

recalls.metaDescription: Igloo recalls 90 Qt. Rolling Coolers due to safety hazards. Contact Igloo for a free replacement handle. Stay safe!

recalls.Description: Black Diamond Equipment has announced a recall for all BD Vision Climbing Harnesses due to a potential fall hazard. These lightweight harnesses, designed for technical alpine and ice climbing, come in various sizes and feature fixed leg loops. The harnesses are available in black and white, with "Black Diamond" printed on one leg loop and "Vision" on the other. All sizes are included in this recall.
recalls.Title: Black Diamond Equipment Issues Recall for BD Vision Climbing Harnesses Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the BD Vision harnesses and reach out to Black Diamond at 866-306-0865 from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email VisionHarnessRecall@bdel.com or visit https://www.blackdiamondequipment.com/en_US/content/product-recalls/ for further details.

Products.Name: BD Vision Climbing Harness
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,580 (In addition, about 130 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Black Diamond Vision Harness
Recalled Black Diamond Vision Harness (label on side of leg loop)

Injuries.Name: Black Diamond has received one report involving the harness waist belt failing. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Liberty Mountain, Recreational Equipment Inc. (REI), other specialty outdoor retailers nationwide, and online at www.blackdiamondequipment.com, www.backcountry.com, and www.evo.com from January 2018 through February 2025, priced around $175.

Importers.Name: Black Diamond Equipment Ltd., of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Potential fall hazard due to degradation over time, risking serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled harnesses and contact Black Diamond for a full refund or a one-time-use $200 credit for future purchases. A pre-paid shipping label will be provided for returning the harness.

recalls.metaDescription: Black Diamond recalls BD Vision Climbing Harnesses due to fall hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for refunds.

recalls.Description: Head Rush Technologies is recalling its TRUBLUE iQ and TRUBLUE iQ+ Auto Belay Devices, which are designed to ensure climbers can descend safely. The TRUBLUE iQ features a gray body with blue handles, while the TRUBLUE iQ+ includes a catch-and-hold brake. Variants of these models are marked with specific tags: the LT versions have blue tags, and the XL versions are identified by purple tags. The recall affects devices with specific serial numbers indicated on their handles and bottoms, which fall within designated ranges for each model.
recalls.Title: Head Rush Technologies Issues Recall for TRUBLUE iQ and TRUBLUE iQ+ Auto Belays Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Head Rush Technologies at 720-565-6885 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email info@headrushtech.com, or visit https://info.headrushtech.com/hubfs/Product_Recalls/SA-0005-v01-en.pdf. More information is also available at www.headrushtech.com by clicking on the 'Recall' banner.

Products.Name: TRUBLUE iQ Auto Belay Device
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 410

Images.Caption: Recalled TruBlue iQ Auto Belay System
Recalled TruBlue iQ+ Auto Belay System
Serial Number Location
Serial Number Location

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 109 reports of incidents of slow retraction, including one injury involving the auto belay not retracting, resulting in a fractured leg.

Manufacturers.Name: Head Rush Technologies, of Louisville, Colorado

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.headrushtech.com from May 2023 to December 2024, priced between $2,800 and $4,600.

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to potential failure to retract

Remedies.Name: Free onsite inspection and repair

recalls.metaDescription: Head Rush Technologies recalls TRUBLUE iQ and iQ+ Auto Belays due to fall hazards. Contact for free inspection and repair.

recalls.Description: ARVA is recalling its NEO BT PRO Avalanche Transceivers due to a potential defect that can lead to a loss of emergency transmission. These devices are black plastic with an LCD screen and feature a red mark button. The serial numbers affected range from NBP00052-1027 to NBP00287-1127 and NB00300-1127 to NB11675-0329, located inside the battery compartment lid.
recalls.Title: ARVA Recalls NEO BT PRO Avalanche Transceivers Due to Emergency Communication Failure Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact ARVA at 844-636-2782 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. MT, Monday to Friday, or visit https://us.arva-equipment.com/content/1488-recall and select "USA" for further details.

Products.Name: ARVA NEO BT PRO Avalanche Transceiver
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,067

Images.Caption: Recalled ARVA NEO BT PRO Avalanche Transceiver
Recalled ARVA NEO BT PRO Avalanche Transceiver
Recalled ARVA NEO BT PRO Avalanche Transceiver

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available at winter outdoor sporting goods and ski/snowboard retailers nationwide, as well as online at arva-equipment.com, backcountry.com, and Evo.com from September 2022 to January 2025, priced around $430.

Importers.Name: Nic Impex ARVA Corp., of Orem, Utah

Hazards.Name: Defective fuse may drain battery power, risking loss of emergency communication and potentially leading to serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the transceiver and reach out to NIC IMPEX ARVA CORP. for a complimentary inspection and repair. A shipping label will be provided for returning the device.

recalls.metaDescription: ARVA recalls NEO BT PRO Avalanche Transceivers due to a risk of losing emergency communication, posing serious injury risks.

recalls.Description: Igloo is recalling its 90 Qt. Flip & Tow Rolling Coolers manufactured before January 2024 due to safety concerns. The coolers feature a tow handle and have "IGLOO" printed on their sides. The manufacturing date can be found on the bottom of the cooler, indicated by a circular imprint. Various color combinations are available for these models, which are included in the recall. A complete list of affected model numbers is available for consumer reference.
recalls.Title: Igloo Recalls Over One Million 90 Quart Rolling Coolers Due to Amputation and Crushing Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Igloo at 888-943-5182 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email igloo90qt@sedgwick.com, or visit www.igloo90qtrecall.expertinquiry.com or http://www.igloocoolers.com and select "Recall Information" for further details.

Products.Name: Igloo 90 Qt. Flip & Tow Rolling Coolers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,060,000 (In addition, about 47,000 in Canada and 23,000 in Mexico)

Images.Caption: Recalled Igloo 90QT Flip & Tow Rolling Cooler in Tactical Gray Body and Deep Slate Stone Lid
Recalled Igloo 90QT Flip & Tow Rolling Cooler in White Body and Lid
Recalled Igloo 90QT Flip & Tow Rolling Cooler in Sapphire Blue Body and Lid
Recalled Igloo 90QT Flip & Tow Rolling Cooler in Dark Slate Blue Body, White Lid

Recalled Igloo 90QT Flip & Tow Rolling Cooler in Carbonite Body, Aegean Sea Lid

Location of Manufacturing Date Code under the recalled Igloo 90Qt Flip & Tow Rolling Cooler

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 12 reports of fingertip injuries, including fingertip amputations, bone fractures, and lacerations.

Manufacturers.Name: Igloo Products Corp., of Katy, Texas

Retailers.Name: Available at Costco, Target, Academy, Dick's, and other retailers nationwide, as well as online at Amazon.com and Igloocoolers.com from January 2019 through January 2025, priced between $80 and $140.

Hazards.Name: The tow handle can pinch consumers' fingertips against the cooler, posing fingertip amputation and crushing hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled coolers and contact Igloo for a free replacement handle.

recalls.metaDescription: Igloo recalls over one million 90 Quart Rolling Coolers due to safety hazards. Contact Igloo for a free replacement handle.

recalls.Description: Setsmart has recalled its LoGest Climbing Ropes due to a potential fall hazard. These ropes, made from hemp, were available in various lengths including 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, and 50 feet. The LoGest Climbing Rope with Carabiner features a silver 'D' ring and a climbing carabiner, while the version with Heavy-Duty Metal Hook includes a blue stripe and a black metal hook secured with silver bolts and nuts. Both types prominently display 'LoGest' in white text on the ends.
recalls.Title: Setsmart Issues Recall for LoGest Climbing Ropes Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Setsmart via email at LoGestRecall@gmail.com or visit www.LoGestRopeRecall.com.

Products.Name: LoGest Climbing Rope with Carabiner
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 22,500 (In addition, about 350 were sold in Canada and about 25 were sold in Mexico)

Images.Caption: Recalled LoGest Climbing Rope with Carabiner
Recalled LoGest Climbing Rope with Heavy-Duty Metal Hook

Injuries.Name: LoGest has received 13 reports of the recalled ropes breaking, including one report of a fall that resulted in a severe spinal cord injury.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from March 2021 to November 2024, priced between $20 and $70.

Importers.Name: Setsmart LLC, of Brooklyn, New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of weakening and breaking, leading to potential falls.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use immediately, uninstall the ropes, and register for a full refund at www.LoGestRopeRecall.com. A disposal form will be provided, which must be signed and submitted along with a photo of the disposed rope to LoGestRecall@gmail.com to process the refund. Refunds will be issued through Amazon to the original payment method. Amazon is notifying all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Setsmart recalls LoGest Climbing Ropes due to fall hazards. Stop using and register for refunds at LoGestRopeRecall.com.

recalls.Description: Black Diamond Equipment has announced a recall for all PIEPS Pro IPS Avalanche Transceivers, which are designed to assist in avalanche emergencies. These transceivers are black with an LCD screen and a yellow mark button, measuring approximately 5 inches long and 3 inches wide. The model name 'PIEPS' is displayed in yellow, while 'PRO IPS' appears in white on the front. Recalled units have serial numbers ranging from 2326 to 2445, located on the label inside the battery compartment door.
recalls.Title: Black Diamond Equipment Issues Recall for PIEPS Pro IPS Avalanche Transceivers Due to Emergency Communication Failure
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Black Diamond at 866-306-0865 from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or email 2024ProIPSrecall@bdel.com. More information is available at https://www.blackdiamondequipment.com/en_US/content/product-recalls/ or by visiting www.blackdiamondequipment.com and selecting 'Product Recalls' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: PIEPS Pro IPS Avalanche Transceiver
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 770 (In addition, about 70 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled PIEPS PRO IPS Avalanche Transceiver - front
Recalled PIEPS PRO IPS Avalanche Transceiver - back
Transceiver's serial number location

Injuries.Name: Black Diamond has received one report of the battery compartment not operating correctly. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Alpenglow Sports, Cripple Creek Backcountry, EVO Gear, Liberty Mountain, Mountain Chalet, Mammoth Mountaineering, and other specialty outdoor retailers nationwide, as well as online at www.backcountry.com, www.polaris.com, and www.blackdiamondequipment.com from February 2024 through November 2024, priced between $530 and $620.

Importers.Name: Black Diamond Equipment Ltd., of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Potential failure of the battery compartment to provide sufficient power, leading to the transceiver not powering on or stopping during use, which could hinder locating a victim in an avalanche and pose serious risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the transceivers immediately and contact Black Diamond for a full refund or complimentary repair, including shipping. No proof of purchase is required, and a pre-paid shipping label will be provided for returning the recalled units. Black Diamond will also reach out to known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Black Diamond recalls PIEPS Pro IPS Avalanche Transceivers due to potential failure in emergency communication. Contact for refunds or repairs.

recalls.Description: Petzl America has issued a recall for three models of climbing harnesses: the GYM harness (Model No. C032AA00), the PANDION harness (Model Nos. C029AA00 and C029AA01), and the PANJI harness (Model No. C028AA00). These harnesses are utilized by climbing gyms, adventure parks, and outdoor enthusiasts. Serial numbers can be found on a label inside each harness, indicating which units are affected by the recall. The serial numbers for the recalled harnesses range from 18319xxxxxx to 19264xxxxxx and include various other sequences for each model. Users are urged to check their harnesses against these numbers to ensure safety.
recalls.Title: Petzl America Recalls GYM, PANDION, and PANJI Climbing Harnesses Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers should contact Petzl America, Inc. at 877-807-3805, available from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday. They can also reach out via email at Returns@petzl.com, or visit https://www.petzl.com/US/en/Operators/safety-alerts for additional details.

Products.Name: Petzl GYM Harness
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 22,000 (In addition, about 5,800 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Petzl GYM Climbing Harness (Model No. C032AA00)
Recalled Petzl PANDION Climbing Harness (Model Nos. C029AA00 and C029AA01)
Recalled Petzl PANJI Climbing Harness (Model No. C028AA00)
Format of serial numbers located on a label inside of the recalled harnesses

Injuries.Name: There has been one report of a user in the United States suffering serious injuries from a fall.

Retailers.Name: Available online and in physical stores at KarstSports, PeakTrading, Backcountry, OmniProGear, Moosejaw, and other outdoor retailers from January 2019 through May 2024, priced between $59.95 and $69.95.

Importers.Name: Petzl America, Inc., of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Risk of falling due to improper harness use, potentially leading to serious injuries or death.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled harnesses immediately and contact Petzl America for a complimentary repair.

recalls.metaDescription: Petzl recalls GYM, PANDION, and PANJI climbing harnesses due to fall hazards. Check your harness serial numbers and contact Petzl for repairs.

recalls.Description: Delta Cycle is recalling its Ceiling Hoists with Straps, model RS2300, which are designed to lift heavy items like bicycles and kayaks using a pulley system. The model number can be found on the product packaging. Consumers should note that if the hoists are used without the straps, no action is necessary. The recall affects units with specific serial numbers listed below or those without a serial number: 65629-66828, 112494-114493, 115494-116502, 131332-132819, 165193-167192, 187360-188895, 220801-222800, 264059-266058, 396421-397428, 428259-430258, 464905-466408, 1650319-1651326, 1667038-1669037, 1717501-1718044, 1750965-1751972, 1776082-1777089. The serial number is located on the underside of the pulley where it attaches to the ceiling.
recalls.Title: Delta Cycle Recalls Ceiling Hoists with Straps Due to Potential Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Delta Cycle Corp at 800-474-6615 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email hoiststraprecall@deltacycle.com, or visit https://designbydelta.com/hoiststraprecall or https://designbydelta.com/ and click on 'Recall on Ceiling Hoist with Straps' for further details.

Products.Name: Ceiling Hoists with Straps
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 25,140 (In addition, about 3,390 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Hoist with Straps holding kayak
Location of strap buckle (view from above)
Location of serial number and strap buckle (view from below)
Location of serial number (if present) on underside of flat part of the ~6" long pulleys
Recalled product packaging showing Model RS2300

Injuries.Name: The firm has received four reports of incidents, including one minor injury.

Retailers.Name: Available at REI, various independent retailers, and online at Amazon.com and designbydelta.com from February 2017 through July 2024 for approximately $40.

Importers.Name: Delta Cycle Corp., of Randolph, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Plastic buckles on the straps may break, creating an injury risk.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the Ceiling Hoist with Straps and contact Delta Cycle Corp for free replacement straps. Cut the old straps with buckles in half and dispose of them. If using the hoist for bicycles without straps, no action is required.

recalls.metaDescription: Delta Cycle recalls Ceiling Hoists with Straps due to injury risk. Check serial numbers and contact for free replacements.

recalls.Description: Head Rush Technologies is recalling its TRUBLUE Speed Auto Belay Devices, available in 12.5m and 16m models. These devices are designed to allow climbers to safely descend after speed climbing. The devices are gray with blue handles; the 12.5m version features a black webbing handgrip above the carabiner, while the 16m version has a blue webbing handgrip. The model number TRUBLUE Speed TBSD150-20 and the serial number can be found on a label located on the handle, with affected serial numbers ranging from TBSD00098 to TBSD01180.
recalls.Title: Head Rush Technologies Recalls TRUBLUE Speed Auto Belay Devices Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should stop using the recalled devices and contact Head Rush Technologies at 720-565-6885 during business hours (8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. MT, Monday to Friday). They can also reach out via email at inf@headrushtech.com or visit https://info.headrushtech.com/hubfs/Product_Recalls/2024_TRUBLUESpeed_Limited_Recall.pdf or www.headrushtech.com and click on the 'Recall' banner for further details.

Products.Name: TRUBLUE Speed Auto Belay Device 12.5m
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 220

Images.Caption: Recalled Head Rush Technologies TRUBLUE Speed Auto Belay Device (12.5 Meter Version)
Recalled Head Rush Technologies TRUBLUE Speed Auto Belay Device (16 Meter Version)
Location of Model Number and Serial Number

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one incident report involving brake failure, causing the user to fall, resulting in a broken ankle.

Manufacturers.Name: Head Rush Technologies, of Louisville, Colorado

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at www.headrushtech.com from May 2017 to July 2021, priced between $2,800 and $2,900.

Hazards.Name: Backup brake failure leading to fall risk and potential serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the devices and contact Head Rush Technologies for a complimentary inspection and repair. The company will reach out to all known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Head Rush Technologies recalls TRUBLUE Speed Auto Belay Devices due to fall hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for inspection.

recalls.Description: Head Rush Technologies is recalling its TRUBLUE iQ Auto Belay Devices, designed to ensure safe descents for climbers. These devices are gray with blue handles, and the TRUBLUE iQ+ features an additional catch-and-hold brake. The LT versions have blue tags above the hand grip, while the XL versions are marked with a purple tag. The recall affects specific serial number ranges for both the iQ and iQ+ models, which are printed on the device's handle and bottom. Please check the serial numbers to see if your device is included in the recall.
recalls.Title: Head Rush Technologies Issues Recall for TRUBLUE iQ Auto Belay Devices Due to Potential Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Head Rush Technologies at 720-565-6885 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email info@headrushtech.com, or visit https://info.headrushtech.com/hubfs/Product_Recalls/2024_TRUBLUEiQ_Voluntary_Recall.pdf or www.headrushtech.com and click on the '2024 TruBlue iQ Voluntary Recall' banner for further details.

Products.Name: TRUBLUE iQ Auto Belay Device
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,200 (In addition, about 254 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled TRUBLU iQ LT Length Version with Gray Strip on Front Cover
Recalled TRUBLU iQ XL Length Version with Purple Tag
Recalled TRUBLU iQ+ LT Length with Catch-and-Hold Brake
Recalled TRUBLU iQ+ XL Length with Catch-and-Hold Brake
Location of Serial Number on Handle of the Recalled TRUBLUE iQ Auto Belay
Location of Serial Number on Bottom of the Recalled TRUBLUE iQ Auto Belay

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 31 reports of incidents involving impaired retraction, including one consumer fall. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Head Rush Technologies, of Louisville, Colorado

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.headrushtech.com from November 2022 to February 2024, priced between $2,800 and $4,600

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall due to potential failure to retract

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled devices and contact Head Rush Technologies for a complimentary inspection and repair

recalls.metaDescription: Head Rush Technologies recalls TRUBLUE iQ Auto Belay Devices due to a fall hazard. Contact them for a free inspection and repair.

recalls.Description: Black Diamond Equipment is recalling Neve Strap Crampons and Accessory Kits, Style No. 400073, with date codes 01-2021, 05-2022, 07-2023, 01-2024, and SS1271. These date codes can be found on the rubber heel strap pull-tab and the underside of the toe piece beneath the anti-balling plate. The crampons, designed for ice climbing, feature aluminum plates with spikes that attach to boots and are characterized by their bright green color along with black plastic toe and heel bails and webbing straps for ankle security. The recall also includes the Neve Strap Binding Accessory Kit, Style No. 400683, and Neve Strap Heel Cup Screw & Nuts, Style No. 400687, which were sold separately as accessories.
recalls.Title: Black Diamond Equipment Recalls Neve Strap Crampons and Accessory Kits Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled Neve Strap Crampons and accessory kits immediately and reach out to Black Diamond Equipment at 866-306-0865 from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday. They can also email NeveStrapRecall@bdel.com or visit https://www.blackdiamondequipment.com/en_US/content/neve-strap-recall/ for further details.

Products.Name: Neve Strap Crampons
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 350 (In addition, about 28 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Neve Strap Crampon
Date code located on the underside of the toe piece of the recalled Neve Strap Crampon

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of incidents of the crampons becoming loose or the heel cup becoming detached by hikers. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Black Diamond stores, REI, and other retailers nationwide, as well as online at blackdiamond.com, backcountry.com, and REI.com from January 2024 through May 2024 for approximately $180.

Importers.Name: Black Diamond Equipment, of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Potential for heel cup detachment due to loose screws, leading to fall risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers will receive a full refund upon returning the products using a prepaid return label provided by Black Diamond Equipment.

recalls.metaDescription: Black Diamond recalls Neve Strap Crampons and Accessory Kits due to a fall hazard. Stop using immediately and contact for a refund.

recalls.Description: Salewa USA is recalling the orange Wild Country 'Ropeman 1' ascenders, which are designed for mountaineering and rock climbing. These products, manufactured in July 2022, are identifiable by the batch number 05A0722 printed in white on the side. The recall is due to a potential failure of the teeth to engage, posing a risk of difficulty or inability to ascend safely.
recalls.Title: Salewa USA Issues Recall for Wild Country Ropeman 1 Ascenders Due to Safety Concerns
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Wild Country at 844-412-7013 from 11 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email Ropeman1Recall@wildcountry.com. Additional information is available online at https://www.wildcountry.com/en-us/product-recalls or by visiting https://www.wildcountry.com/en-us/ and selecting 'Ropeman 1 Voluntary Recall' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Wild Country Ropeman 1 Ascenders
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 7,700 (In addition, about 297 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Wild Country Ropeman 1 Ascender
Recalled Wild Country Ropeman 1 Ascender

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available at REI and other specialty outdoor retailers across the country, as well as online at www.wildcountry.com, www.backcountry.com, and www.campsaver.com from December 2022 through April 2024 for approximately $55.

Importers.Name: Salewa USA LLC, of Boulder, Colorado

Hazards.Name: Potential failure of teeth to engage, hindering ascent.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled Ropeman 1 ascenders immediately and contact Wild Country for a return and free replacement. Alternatively, you can return the product directly to Wild Country or any authorized dealer for a free replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: Salewa USA recalls Wild Country Ropeman 1 ascenders due to safety issues. Stop using them and contact Wild Country for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Anker is recalling its EverFrost Coolers due to a potential fire hazard linked to the lithium-ion battery packs. These coolers, designed for outdoor activities such as camping and fishing, feature a dark gray color with wheels and a drop-down handle. They include a blue charging port for alternative power sources and display the name 'Anker EverFrost' in silver and blue on the side. The model numbers affected by this recall include Anker Detachable Battery Pack for Powered Cooler A17B0, Anker EverFrost Powered Coolers 30 A17A0, Anker EverFrost Powered Coolers 40 A17A1, and Anker EverFrost Powered Coolers 50 A17A2.
recalls.Title: Recall of Anker EverFrost Lithium-Ion Battery Powered Coolers Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Anker at 800-988-7973 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, email support@anker.com, or visit https://support.anker.com/s/emailcontactus. A LiveChat option is available at https://support.anker.com/s/contact-us, and further details can be found at https://www.anker.com/anker-solix/cooler-battery-recall-pps.

Products.Name: Anker EverFrost Battery Powered Cooler
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 5,480 (In addition, about 160 in Canada)

Packaging of recalled EverFrost Battery Powered Cooler Model A17A0

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of overheating, smoke and/or fire. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Anker Innovations Limited, of Hong Kong

Retailers.Name: Available at Best Buy, TD Synnex, and other retailers nationwide, as well as online at kickstarter.com from May 2023 to September 2023, priced between $250 and $950.

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to overheating lithium-ion batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the coolers and contact Anker for a free replacement battery pack. Dispose of recalled batteries according to local laws.

recalls.metaDescription: Anker recalls EverFrost Coolers due to fire risks from lithium-ion batteries. Stop using and contact Anker for a free replacement.

recalls.Description:
Cascade Designs has issued a recall for specific MSR camping cooking pots due to safety concerns. These anodized aluminum pots, available in 1- to 2-liter sizes, were sold individually and as part of various stove systems and kits. The affected models include Reactor, Trail Lite, and PocketRocket Stove Kits, all marked with 'MSR' on the side.

The primary hazard is that the pot handle may detach or loosen, which can lead to burns or scalds from hot food and liquids spilling out. Consumers are urged to take immediate action to ensure their safety.
recalls.Title: Cascade Designs Recalls MSR Camping Cooking Pots Due to Burn and Scald Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Cascade Designs Inc. at 800-531-9531 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.msrgear.com/pot-recall.html. You can also access the recall information by clicking on the 'Recall Header' on their website at https://www.msrgear.com.

Products.Name: MSR Reactor Camping Pot
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 60,655 (In addition, about 22,320 were sold in Canada and about 35 were sold in Mexico)

Images.Caption: Recalled MSR Reactor Accessory cooking pot (1 liter)
Recalled MSR Trail Lite pot (2 liter)
Recalled MSR Reactor Stove System (1.7 liter)
Recalled MSR PocketRocket Stove Kit

Injuries.Name: The company has received 22 reports of the pot handle either detaching or becoming loose, including two reports of burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at outdoor equipment retailers across the country from January 2008 to October 2023, priced between $40 and $290.

Importers.Name: Cascade Designs Inc., of Seattle, Washington

Distributors.Name: Cascade Designs Inc., of Seattle, Washington

Hazards.Name: Potential for handle detachment leading to burns and scalds

Remedies.Name: Stop using the pot and contact Cascade Designs for a free replacement handle/lifter, which is easy to install without tools.

recalls.metaDescription: Cascade Designs recalls MSR camping cooking pots due to burn risks. Contact for a free replacement handle and ensure your safety.

recalls.Description: FeraDyne Outdoors has announced a recall of their Rhino branded 24-foot Hot Foot Climbing Sticks, which are designed for game hunting. These climbing sticks feature extended tree braces with angled steps and consist of six four-foot sections. The model number 'RTSL-24' along with lot codes can be found on a label attached to the support pole. This recall affects all lot codes from the calendar year 2023 and earlier, formatted as a two-digit year followed by a two-digit month, such as 2312xx or 2212xx.
recalls.Title: FeraDyne Outdoors Issues Recall for Rhino 24-foot Hot Foot Climbing Sticks Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled climbing sticks immediately and contact FeraDyne Outdoors at 800-787-7589, available Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT. Alternatively, they can email RTSL24recall@FeraDyne.com or visit www.RTSL24recall.com or www.feradyne.com and click on 'Recall' for further details.

Products.Name: Rhino 24-foot Hot Foot Climbing Sticks
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 8,900

Images.Caption: Recalled Rhino 24-foot Hot Foot Climbing Stick, Model RTSL-24

Injuries.Name: FeraDyne has received nine reports of the recalled climbing sticks breaking, resulting in three reports of laceration injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, and various sporting goods retailers across the nation from January 2021 to December 2023 for approximately $100.

Importers.Name: FeraDyne Outdoors, LLC, of Superior, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of breaking at the weld point, leading to fall and laceration dangers.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the climbing sticks and contact FeraDyne for a full refund. They can return the product using a prepaid mailer provided by FeraDyne or receive guidance on how to properly dispose of the climbing stick.

recalls.metaDescription: FeraDyne recalls Rhino 24-foot Hot Foot Climbing Sticks due to fall and laceration hazards. Contact for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description: FeraDyne Outdoors is recalling the Treestand Safety Harnesses with model numbers RTH-2000S and RTH-2001S, which were packaged with various Rhino Tree Stands and Ladder Stands. The affected models include RTH-100, RTH-200, RTL-100, RTL-200, RTL-300, RTL-400, RTL-1000, RTL-3000, and RTL-4000. These safety harnesses feature black cloth strips with a white tether, stitched with orange thread, and the model numbers are labeled accordingly. The recall is due to a failure to meet the ASTM F2337-21 safety standard, which can lead to stitching failure and potential falls. Consumers should take immediate action to ensure their safety.
recalls.Title: FeraDyne Outdoors Issues Recall for Rhino Tree Stands Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact FeraDyne Outdoors at 800-787-7589, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT, or email rhinostandsrecall@feradyne.com. More information can be found online at www.rhinotreestandsrecall.com or by visiting www.feradyne.com and selecting 'Recall Information' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Rhino Tree Stand Safety Harness
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 50,000 (In addition, about 340 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled FeraDyne Outdoors Rhino Tree Stand Safety Harness
Recalled FeraDyne Outdoors Rhino Tree Stand stitching

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available at Dick's Merchandising & Supply, Orscheln Farm & Home, and various hunting and supply stores across the country, as well as online at Amazon.com and other retailers from February 2021 through December 2022, priced between $110 and $360.

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with safety standards leading to potential fall risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled safety harness and contact FeraDyne Outdoors for a free replacement harness. Proof of destruction of the original harness is required.

recalls.metaDescription: FeraDyne Outdoors recalls Rhino Tree Stands due to safety harness fall hazards. Immediate action required for replacement.

recalls.Description: The recall pertains to SABRE Aim & Fire Pepper Gel Spray with Training Canisters, model number SDP-G-03. These canisters resemble a toy gun, featuring a black handle and an orange body. Only those units with an expiration date of 9/2027 are affected, which can be found on the white label affixed to the side of the canisters.
recalls.Title: SABRE Aim & Fire Pepper Gel Spray Canisters Recall Due to Malfunction in Emergencies
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Security Equipment Corp. at 800-325-9568 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email customersupport@sabredred.com, or visit www.saberered.com and click on 'Aim & Fire Pepper Spray Recall' or go to https://www.sabrered.com/2023-aim-and-fire-pepper-gel-recall/ for further details.

Products.Name: SABRE Aim & Fire Pepper Gel Spray with Training Canisters
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 4,000

Images.Caption: Recalled SABRE Aim & Fire Pepper Gel Spray with Training Canister
Red nozzle on top of the recalled SABRE Aim & Fire Pepper Gel Canister

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the red nozzle missing. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Security Equipment Corporation, of Fenton, Missouri

Retailers.Name: Available at sporting goods stores across the country and online at Amazon.com between July 2023 and August 2023 for approximately $50.

Hazards.Name: The canisters lack the necessary red nozzle on the valve stem for deployment, rendering them ineffective in emergencies.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled canisters immediately and reach out to Security Equipment Corporation for guidance on obtaining a free replacement, including a prepaid shipping label for the return.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of SABRE Aim & Fire Pepper Gel Spray Canisters due to failure to operate in emergencies. Contact for free replacement.
